NKE.reasoning: Catalyst window: No identifiable catalyst within 10 trading days. Q4 FY2026 earnings are June 25, 2026 (~39 trading days out), well outside the catalyst-trading window. FOMC decision is today (April 29) but is a macro event, not NKE-specific. Net news sentiment: overwhelmingly negative — Piper Sandler/DA Davidson/Citigroup downgrades in April 2026, Chief Innovation Officer departure, Q3 revenues down 9.8% YoY, no sales growth recovery expected until February 2027. Insider activity: quiet in current data window; prior wiki data shows board buying at ~$42-43 in early April (Robert Swan $500K, John Rogers $173K) which is a contrarian signal, but net 90-trade flow is still sell-side heavy ($19.7M buys vs. $33.2M sells). Technical setup: EMA aligned downtrend across all timeframes; price 14% below 50-SMA and 24% below 200-EMA. Hourly OBV trending down, hourly MACD bearish, price below all hourly MAs. MACD histogram on daily is improving (+0.52) and daily OBV is diverging positively from price, suggesting some internal accumulation, but the overall structure remains in a distribution phase. Without a near-term catalyst to act as a thesis anchor, this setup does not meet the catalyst-trading entry criteria regardless of the contrarian board-buying signal.

DIS.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q2 FY2026 earnings expected ~May 6-13, 2026, approximately 5-10 trading days out — squarely in the catalyst-trading window. This is the primary thesis anchor. Net news sentiment: mixed-to-positive. Positive items: Q1 FY2026 was a beat ($1.63 EPS vs. $1.57 estimate, streaming income +72% YoY); Disney+/Hulu/Max bundle launch; Disney-Epic Games partnership; international Sky streaming deal. Negative/mixed: 1,000 job cuts in April 2026 including Marvel Studios roles (margin-positive but content-quality risk); Netflix competitive pressure at 325M subscribers. Net sentiment leans positive given fundamental momentum into earnings. Insider activity: Wiki records April 2026 net 88 buys vs. 71 sells — net positive; board RSU grants at ~$96.96 in March establish institutional cost basis well below current price. Technical setup: Stock pulled back approximately 5% from recent high of $107.11 to current $101.30, sitting directly on the volume-confirmed daily support at $101.02 (15 tests). This is a constructive pre-catalyst pullback to support rather than an exhausted setup. Daily MACD is positive (line 0.90 vs. signal 0.785) with a positive histogram. Daily ADX 34.88 indicates trend strength. Bollinger pct_b at 0.526 — midrange, not overbought. Daily RSI-21 at 69.96 reflects the prior run-up, but the current pullback to support has reset near-term momentum. Hourly caution: hourly ADX shows strong bearish pressure (minus_di 31 vs. plus_di 13), suggesting the pullback may have a few more hours to run — consider waiting for hourly stabilization at $100.61-$101.02 support zone before entry. Risk/reward: entry ~$101.50 near support, target ~$107.00 (prior resistance, ~5.4% catalyst-magnitude move consistent with earnings for a mega-cap), stop at $100.20 below volume-confirmed hourly support at $100.61. R:R approximately 2.5:1. Key risk: earnings miss on streaming margin (Q1 was 8.4% vs. 10% target) or Parks softness would negate the setup.
